layers.name: cmd
rules: Wire dependencies together (LLM, storage, tools, agent). No business logic.
No direct LLM calls. No AST parsing. Delegate all work to agents, runner, and tools.

layers.name: tools
rules: Each tool must be self-contained and deterministic.
Only use Go standard library packages (go/ast, go/parser, go/token, os, strings, etc.).
No LLM calls. No agent dependencies. No shared mutable state.
Execute must return a result or a JSON error string — never return a Go error for tool-level failures.

layers.name: agents
rules: Orchestration layer. Manages the plan-act loop between the LLM and tools.
No direct database access. No HTTP calls outside of the llm interface.
Must not import specific tool implementations — depend on the Tool interface only.

layers.name: runner
rules: Runs the agent plan-act loop. No business logic of its own.
Only calls agent.Plan and agent.Act in a loop until the agent signals completion.
No direct LLM or storage access.

layers.name: stores
rules: Persistence only. Implements the Store interface for message history.
No LLM calls. No agent logic. No tool imports.

layers.name: llms
rules: LLM client implementations only. Handles HTTP communication with model backends.
No agent logic. No tool imports. No storage access.
Must implement the Generate interface and return structured responses.
